entries.title: Floating U.A. Is A Mobile Prison Battlefield

context.label: Floating U.A. battle begins against Shigaraki

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Floating U.A. is built to contain Shigaraki away from civilians while support systems and Erasure suppress his Quirks.
content.injection: Use Floating U.A. as a fragile engineered battlefield; its supports, barriers, propulsion, and Erasure logistics matter.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia
sourceInfo.notes: Draft Saga Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

entries.title: Shigaraki Mutates Without Active Quirks

context.label: Shigaraki mutation escalates under Erasure

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Even under Erasure, Shigaraki’s body produces overwhelming hand-like growths and physical pressure that are not treated as ordinary active Quirk use.
content.injection: Do not have Erasure fully neutralize Shigaraki; it suppresses Quirks but does not make him a normal human opponent.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia

entries.title: AFO And Shigaraki Identity Remains Unstable

context.label: Shigaraki mutation escalates under Erasure

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: During the Floating U.A. fight, Shigaraki’s body and will are entangled with All For One’s control, but Tomura’s self is not gone forever.
content.injection: Keep the Shigaraki/AFO identity unstable; avoid portraying him as only AFO or only independent Tomura before the relevant shifts occur.

content.fact: Aizawa, Monoma, and support personnel keep Shigaraki’s active Quirks suppressed through a fragile support chain.

entries.title: Big Three Buy Time Against Shigaraki

context.label: The Big Three hold the line at Floating U.A.

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Mirio, Tamaki, and Nejire cannot simply defeat Shigaraki, but their attacks and misdirection buy time for Deku’s arrival.
content.injection: Use the Big Three as high-skill delay fighters; their role is courage and tactical pressure, not an easy win.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia

content.injection: Make Suneater’s peak attack costly, prepared, and insufficient by itself against Shigaraki’s scale.

context.label: The Big Three hold the line at Floating U.A.

content.fact: Mirio’s humor and emotional jabs can briefly disrupt Shigaraki’s composure, but cannot solve the battle alone.

entries.title: Bakugo Suffers A Near-Fatal Battlefield State

content.fact: Bakugo is gravely injured at Floating U.A., forcing the battlefield into crisis before his later return.

context.label: Floating U.A. systems fail under pressure

content.fact: Floating U.A. depends on support-course engineering, power, and external help; damage can make the battlefield fail.
content.injection: Keep support infrastructure visible in Floating U.A. scenes; the battlefield is a machine under attack, not just scenery.

entries.title: Deku Must Control Rage On Arrival

context.label: Deku reaches Floating U.A.

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: When Deku arrives and sees the battlefield damage, he risks losing focus and must be grounded before fighting Shigaraki effectively.
content.injection: Write Deku’s anger as dangerous but redirected; he should fight with purpose, not blind berserker rage.

entries.title: Gearshift Is A Final War Trump Card

context.label: Deku uses Gearshift against Shigaraki

continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Gearshift lets Deku alter motion at extreme levels, but its use has severe time and body consequences.
content.injection: Do not make Gearshift a consequence-free speed buff; after use, Deku is on a narrowing timer.

content.fact: When Deku first arrives at Floating U.A., he does not yet know the full depth of Tenko’s origin or All For One’s manipulation.
